text.title: ruby-rspec-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ruby-rspec-testing. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: One describe per public method, one context per branch, one it per assertion.
rule.rationale: Keeps failure messages diagnostic: "Users::CreateService#call with invalid params does not create a user" pinpoints the failing behavior without reading the test body.

rule.statement: Name subject explicitly: subject(:service) { described_class.new(params: params) }.
rule.rationale: Unnamed subject { described_class.new } is unreadable when nested three contexts deep.

rule.statement: Use instance_double not double for service/repository fakes.
rule.rationale: instance_double verifies the method actually exists on the class; double lets agents stub nonexistent methods silently.

rule.statement: Prefer build_stubbed over create whenever no DB write is required.
rule.rationale: build_stubbed skips the database entirely; model unit tests are 10-100x faster with no schema dependency.

rule.statement: Avoid let! unless setup must run regardless of reference; prefer lazy let.
rule.rationale: let! triggers a DB write unconditionally; lazy let only writes when the variable is referenced, keeping tests that do not need the record fast.

rule.statement: Mirror the source-tree structure in spec/: spec/services/users/create_service_spec.rb for app/services/users/create_service.rb.
rule.rationale: SimpleCov groups coverage by file path; mismatched paths break per-file gap reporting.

rule.statement: Use :transaction strategy for all non-JS specs; use :truncation only for Capybara JS specs.
rule.rationale: Transaction rollback is the fastest cleanup; Capybara JS specs spawn a browser thread that cannot share the DB transaction, so truncation is required.

rule.statement: Standardize the strategy per spec type via RSpec.configure, never per individual spec.
rule.rationale: Per-spec overrides create inconsistency; agents copying the nearest before block apply the wrong strategy and introduce intermittent failures.

rule.statement: Pin time via ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers travel_to with the block form; always travel_back after.
rule.rationale: Without the block form, time stays frozen for subsequent specs. travel_to { ... } guarantees cleanup automatically.

rule.statement: Stub external HTTP with WebMock; record cassettes with VCR against staging; review cassette diffs on every API update.
rule.rationale: VCR replays old cassettes silently even when the API contract changed; periodic VCR_RECORD_MODE=all reruns expose drift.

rule.statement: Use :transaction strategy for all non-JS specs; use :truncation only for Capybara JS specs.
rule.rationale: Transaction rollback is the fastest cleanup. Capybara JS specs spawn a browser thread that cannot share the DB transaction.

rule.statement: Standardize the strategy per spec type via RSpec.configure, never per individual spec.
rule.rationale: Per-spec overrides create inconsistency. Agents copying the nearest before block apply the wrong strategy and introduce intermittent failures.

rule.statement: Pin time via ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers travel_to with the block form; always travel_back after.
rule.rationale: Without the block form, time stays frozen for subsequent specs. travel_to { ... } guarantees cleanup automatically.

rule.statement: Stub external HTTP with WebMock; record cassettes with VCR against staging; review cassette diffs on every API update.
rule.rationale: VCR replays old cassettes silently even when the API contract changed. Periodic VCR_RECORD_MODE=all reruns expose drift.

rule.statement: Prefer build_stubbed over create whenever possible. No DB write; specs are 10-100x faster for model unit tests.
rule.rationale: Schema dependencies and DB latency are the primary scaling bottleneck in test suites.

rule.statement: One describe per public method, context per branch, it per assertion. Keeps failure messages diagnostic.
rule.rationale: Diagnostic output reduces debugging friction.

rule.statement: Avoid let! unless setup must run regardless of usage. Lazy let is the default; eager triggers DB writes you do not need.
rule.rationale: Unnecessary DB writes slow the suite without improving test isolation.

rule.statement: subject named (subject(:service)); unnamed subject makes specs unreadable when nested.
rule.rationale: Readability improves debugging and agent spec generation.

rule.statement: Use instance_double not double. Verifies the method actually exists on the SUT.
rule.rationale: Agents can stub nonexistent methods with double, creating false-positive tests.

rule.statement: Mock at the boundary, not internals. Mock the HTTP gateway, not the service that calls the gateway. Otherwise refactor breaks tests.
rule.rationale: Tests should verify behavior contracts, not implementation details.

rule.statement: Use shared examples for cross-cutting behavior (auth, pagination); use shared contexts for setup blocks. Do not mix.
rule.rationale: Semantic separation keeps intent clear and reduces copy-paste.

rule.statement: System specs use Cuprite headless Chrome. Selenium is historical default — slower and flakier.
rule.rationale: Cuprite uses Chrome DevTools Protocol directly, eliminating WebDriver overhead.

rule.statement: Run bundle exec rspec --bisect after any flake. Order-dependence is debt that compounds.
rule.rationale: Test ordering bugs are exponentially harder to debug as the suite grows.

rule.statement: Mutation testing on critical services (auth, billing). Coverage alone hides assertion-free specs.
rule.rationale: Coverage metrics count lines executed, not assertions verified. Mutation testing forces true test quality.

rule.statement: Tag specs with metadata (:slow, :integration, :flaky_known); CI selects subsets per stage.
rule.rationale: Allows fast feedback loops (run fast specs) without sacrificing comprehensive CI.
